questions.scenario: A pull request says it gives the orders app permission to write invoice exports. The policy grants admin access to all storage. What should a reviewer do?

options.text: Pause approval because the file diff is broader than the story.
Approve because any working permission is fine.
Ignore permissions because IaC handles security automatically.

questions.explanation: IaC review should ask whether the proposed infrastructure matches the stated operational need.

questions.scenario: A change opens HTTPS to the public load balancer. Which reviewer question fits the IaC habit?

options.text: Is only the intended public entry point exposed?
Can we avoid documenting the change?
Can the database also be public for convenience?
Should this be done in the console after merge?

questions.explanation: Review turns infrastructure into team knowledge before real exposure changes.

questions.scenario: Which pieces of evidence help review an infrastructure change before apply?

options.text: The plan or dry-run output when available.
Only a verbal promise that it works.

questions.explanation: The safest review compares the human story, the file diff, and tool evidence.
